Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: Powder recycling for electron beam powder bed fusion of TiAl alloy

Composition_Info.Note: 实测成分仅给出Al含量（48.43 at%），其他元素未测量。氧含量见Properties_Info。

Process_Text.original: Ti-48Al-2Cr-2Nb powders, produced by EIGA with particle size ranging from 53–150 μm were supplied by the Institute of Metal Research, Chinese Academy of Sciences. New powders were labeled as R0.

Microstructure_Text.original: Initially, the new powders consisted of γ and α/α2 phases primarily, with the distinction between α and α2 phases being challenging via XRD. Similar to findings by Guyon et al. [25] on Ti-48Al-2Cr-2Nb powders, which used ordered “super-α2” peaks to distinguish α/α2 phases, the results by comparing the characteristic peaks showed that the initial powders were dominated by disordered α and γ phases.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Virgin powder mainly composed of disordered α and γ phases.

Composition_Info.Note: 实测成分仅给出Al含量（48.47 at%），其他元素未测量。

Process_Text.original: After exposure to high temperatures and cooled to RT, both the powders in the hoppers and the un-melted powders were processed in the powder recovery system (PRS). The resulting blended powders were sieved, mixed, stored, and reloaded. The first recycled powders as R1.
Process_Text.simplified: First recycling cycle: powders exposed to EB-PBF thermal cycle, then recovered, sieved and mixed.

Microstructure_Text.original: In powders from R1 to R15, the α/α2 phase gradually decreased with increasing recycling number, and the interdendritic γ-region expanded.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: α/α2 phase decreases, γ phase increases.

Composition_Info.Note: 实测成分仅给出Al含量（48.32 at%），其他元素未测量。

Microstructure_Text.original: In powders from R1 to R15, the α/α2 phase gradually decreased with increasing recycling number, and the interdendritic γ-region expanded.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: α/α2 phase decreases, γ phase increases.

Composition_Info.Note: 实测成分仅给出Al含量（47.24 at%），其他元素未测量。

Microstructure_Text.original: By R30, the powder was predominantly composed of the γ phase, with S-segregation disappeared due to the high diffusion rate of Al at temperatures of approximately 900-1150°C. In powders from R1 to R15, the α/α2 phase gradually decreased.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: γ phase becomes dominant, α/α2 decreases.

Composition_Info.Note: 实测成分仅给出Al含量（46.81 at%），其他元素未测量。

Microstructure_Text.original: By R30, the powder was predominantly composed of the γ phase, with S-segregation disappeared. The bright dendritic structure caused by β segregation transformed into a block-like morphology (α2).
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Predominantly γ phase, β segregation transformed to blocky α2.

Composition_Info.Note: 实测成分仅给出Al含量（47.86 at%），其他元素未测量。这是添加新鲜粉末后的混合粉末。

Process_Text.original: After 30 cycles, 10kg of new powders were mixed. Following remixing (termed R30b). Post-mixing, the new powders comprised approximately 30.3% of the mixture.
Process_Text.simplified: 30 cycles followed by addition of 30.3% fresh powder.

Microstructure_Text.original: After adding new powders, three structures were observed. Some powder particles lost their dendritic structure completely, showing dispersed bright spots enriched with Cr.

Composition_Info.Note: 实测成分仅给出Al含量（47.12 at%），其他元素未测量。

Microstructure_Text.original: R45-R70 powders exhibited semi-molten agglomerates with elongated structures. Irregular particles increased.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Increased irregular particles and semi-molten agglomerates.

Composition_Info.Note: 实测成分仅给出Al含量（46.31±3 at%），其他元素未测量。

Microstructure_Text.original: In R70 powders, irregularly shaped particles increased, along with powder particles containing aggregated bright spots. Microstructures of irregular powders were non-uniform due to varying Al loss. XPS demonstrated progressive transition from mixed TiO2/Al2O3 to Al2O3 dominated (82.11%) surface oxides after 70 cycles. TEM revealed Cr-Ti Laves phases and Ti-Nb-Cr precipitates.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: High irregular particle fraction, Al2O3-dominated surface oxide, C14 Laves phase precipitates.

Process_Text.original: The Ti-48Al-2Cr-2Nb parts were fabricated using EB-PBF equipment (Qbeam S200, Beijing Quick Beam Technology Co.) with a constant accelerating voltage of 60kV. The layer thickness was 50μm and the powder-bed temperature ranged from 900°C to 1150°C during building. Materialise Magics (version 24.0) was used for slice processing and model creation. The dense building process for thin-walled parts is detailed in Table 1: Substrate preheating temperature 1070°C, powder bed preheating temperature 900-1150°C, building temperature 900-1100°C, layer thickness 50μm, scanning speed 1.8-2.3 m/s, mean beam current 5.4-8.6 mA, line offset 0.1 mm, energy density 1.80-2.24 J/mm².
Process_Text.simplified: EB-PBF using Qbeam S200, 60kV, layer thickness 50μm, powder bed temperature 900-1150°C, building temperature 900-1100°C, scanning speed 1.8-2.3 m/s, beam current 5.4-8.6 mA, line offset 0.1 mm, energy density 1.80-2.24 J/mm².

Process_Info.Equipment: Qbeam S200, Beijing Quick Beam Technology Co.

Microstructure_Text.original: XRD and SEM analyses of S0 revealed γ and α2 phases, with equiaxed γ grains, fine lamellar colonies (α2+γ), and dispersed bright spots (β0/B2) along lamellar colony boundaries. EBSD revealed pronounced γ bands and annealed twins. TEM showed dual-phase structure of lamellar colonies (α2+γ) and equiaxed γ grains, with cubic β0/B2 phase between lamellar colonies and γ grain boundaries.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: γ and α2 phases, equiaxed γ grains, lamellar colonies, β0/B2 precipitates at boundaries, γ bands with annealing twins.

Properties_Info.Note: From Fig. 10(a) caption and text: 'S0 exhibiting the highest plasticity' and strength value.

Process_Text.original: Same as S0, using recycled powder after 5 cycles.
Process_Text.simplified: EB-PBF with same parameters, powder recycled 5 times.

Microstructure_Text.original: S5 exhibited distinct 'y bands' with annealed twins.

Process_Text.original: Same as S0, using recycled powder after 10 cycles.
Process_Text.simplified: EB-PBF with same parameters, powder recycled 10 times.

Microstructure_Text.original: S10 exhibited distinct 'y bands' with annealed twins.

Process_Text.original: Same as S0, using recycled powder after 15 cycles.
Process_Text.simplified: EB-PBF with same parameters, powder recycled 15 times.

Microstructure_Text.original: S15 showed more pronounced lamellar clusters, and elimination of large-scale γ-band structures enhanced microstructural homogeneity. EBSD revealed grain size <10 μm, LAGB 7.40%, and special boundaries in γ [111]60° plane. TEM showed dual-phase structure with C15 Laves phase precipitates at boundaries and within α2 regions.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Fine grains (<10 μm), lamellar clusters, no γ bands, Laves phase precipitates.

Properties_Info.Note: Extracted from Fig. 10(a) inset table. S15 has the highest ductility.

Process_Text.original: Same as S0, using recycled powder after 30 cycles.
Same as S0, using recycled powder after 30 cycles.
Process_Text.simplified: EB-PBF with same parameters, powder recycled 30 times.

Microstructure_Text.original: S30 exhibited β0/B2 phase peaks in XRD, bright spots (β0/B2) along lamellar colony boundaries and inside γ bulk. EBSD revealed γ bands and increased β0/B2 phase content.

Process_Text.original: Same as S0, using recycled powder after 30 cycles with addition of fresh powder (R30b).
Process_Text.simplified: EB-PBF with same parameters, powder recycled 30 times then mixed with 30.3% fresh powder.

Microstructure_Text.original: S30b exhibited γ bands reappeared, and more pronounced lamellar clusters. Elongation improved compared to S30.

Process_Text.original: Same as S0, using recycled powder after 45 cycles.
Process_Text.simplified: EB-PBF with same parameters, powder recycled 45 times.

Microstructure_Text.original: S45 showed more pronounced lamellar clusters, and β/Laves phase peaks in XRD. γ bands barely visible.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Lamellar clusters, B2/Laves phases, few γ bands.

Measured_Composition.Measurement_Method: Table 3 (calculated from S-Fig 8 and text values)

Composition_Info.Note: Values calculated based on data provided in the caption of Fig. 10 and S-Fig. 8 for S70, as Table 3 is incomplete for this sample.

Process_Text.original: Same as S0, using recycled powder after 70 cycles.
Process_Text.simplified: EB-PBF with same parameters, powder recycled 70 times.

Microstructure_Text.original: S70 exhibited highest strength but brittle fracture. EBSD showed β0/β2 phase content 4.6%, grain size <10 μm with even finer grains, LAGB 8.15%, twin boundaries decreased to 5.61%. TEM revealed C14 Laves phase precipitates 2-3 μm in size (TiNbCr4). Microstructure showed spheroidized and agglomerated β0/B2 phase, γ bands barely visible.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Fine grains (<10 μm, many <5 μm), 4.6% B2 phase, C14 Laves precipitates (2-3 μm), low twin boundary fraction (5.61%), high LAGB (8.15%).

Properties_Info.Note: From Fig. 10(a) data and text: 'S70 exhibiting the highest strength'.
From text: '60% reduction from 2.1%' and Fig. 10(a) inset table.
From S-Fig. 8 caption which provides data for S70, as Table 3 does not list it.
